records.ety: [FF. dposer, in the sense of L. deponere to put down; but from pref. d- (L. de) + poser to place. See Pose, Pause.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deposed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Deposing.]

defs: To lay down; to divest one's self of; to lay aside.
quotes: Thus when the state one Edward did depose, A greater Edward in his room arose.

defs: To remove from a throne or other high station; to dethrone; to divest or deprive of office.
quotes: A tyrant over his subjects, and therefore worthy to be deposed.

defs: To testify under oath; to bear testimony to; -- now usually said of bearing testimony which is officially written down for future use.
quotes: To depose the yearly rent or valuation of lands.
Depose him in the justice of his cause.

defs: To bear witness; to testify under oath; to make deposition.
quotes: Then, seeing't was he that made you to despose, Your oath, my lord, is vain and frivolous.
